channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Two-dimensional early exit optimisation of LLM inference
item.description: arXiv:2604.18592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a two-dimensional (2D) early exit strategy that coordinates layer-wise and sentence-wise exiting for classification tasks in large language models. By processing input incrementally sentence-by-sentence while progressively activating deep...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, arxiv, large language model, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scripts Through Time: A Survey of the Evolving Role of Transliteration in NLP
item.description: arXiv:2604.18722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual transfer in NLP is often hindered by the ``script barrier'' where differences in writing systems inhibit transfer learning between languages. Transliteration, the process of converting the script, has emerged as a powerful technique to b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, research, analysis, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Investigating Counterfactual Unfairness in LLMs towards Identities through Humor
item.description: arXiv:2604.18729v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humor holds up a mirror to social perception: what we find funny often reflects who we are and how we judge others. When language models engage with humor, their reactions expose the social assumptions they have internalized from training data. In thi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, experiment, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Syntax as a Rosetta Stone: Universal Dependencies for In-Context Coptic Translation
item.description: arXiv:2604.18758v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-resource machine translation requires methods that differ from those used for high-resource languages. This paper proposes a novel in-context learning approach to support low-resource machine translation of the Coptic language to English, with syn...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, model, instruction, in-context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Empirical Study of Multi-Generation Sampling for Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.18775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting jailbreak behaviour in large language models remains challenging, particularly when strongly aligned models produce harmful outputs only rarely. In this work, we present an empirical study of output based jailbreak detection under realistic ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, large language model, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mango: Multi-Agent Web Navigation via Global-View Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.18779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing web agents typically initiate exploration from the root URL, which is inefficient for complex websites with deep hierarchical structures. Without a global view of the website's structure, agents frequently fall into navigation traps, explore ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, model, RAG, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On Solving the Multiple Variable Gapped Longest Common Subsequence Problem
item.description: arXiv:2604.18645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper addresses the Variable Gapped Longest Common Subsequence (VGLCS) problem, a generalization of the classical LCS problem involving flexible gap constraints between consecutive solutions' characters. The problem arises in molecular sequence c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, paper, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ARES: Adaptive Red-Teaming and End-to-End Repair of Policy-Reward System
item.description: arXiv:2604.18789v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) is central to aligning Large Language Models (LLMs), yet it introduces a critical vulnerability: an imperfect Reward Model (RM) can become a single point of failure when it fails to penalize unsafe beh...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RLHF, fine-tuning, large language model, prompt, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI scientists produce results without reasoning scientifically
item.description: arXiv:2604.18805v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are increasingly deployed to conduct scientific research autonomously, yet whether their reasoning adheres to the epistemic norms that make scientific inquiry self-correcting is poorly understood. Here, we eval...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, research, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Human-Guided Harm Recovery for Computer Use Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.18847v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LM agents gain the ability to execute actions on real computer systems, we need ways to not only prevent harmful actions at scale but also effectively remediate harm when prevention fails. We formalize a solution to this neglected challenge in post...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, model, context, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Natural Language to Executable Narsese: A Neuro-Symbolic Benchmark and Pipeline for Reasoning with NARS
item.description: arXiv:2604.18873v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are highly capable at language generation, but they remain unreliable when reasoning requires explicit symbolic structure, multi-step inference, and interpretable uncertainty. This paper presents a neuro-symbolic framework...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, paper, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Formally Verified Patent Analysis via Dependent Type Theory: Machine-Checkable Certificates from a Hybrid AI + Lean 4 Pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2604.18882v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a formally verified framework for patent analysis as a hybrid AI + Lean 4 pipeline. The DAG-coverage core (Algorithm 1b) is fully machine-verified once bounded match scores are fixed. Freedom-to-operate, claim-construction sensitivity, cros...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, analysis, framework, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Easy Samples Are All You Need: Self-Evolving LLMs via Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.18639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Previous LLMs-based RL studies typically follow either supervised learning with high annotation costs, or unsupervised paradigms using voting or entropy-based rewards. However, their performance remains far from satisfactory due to the substantial ann...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, model, reasoning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Discrete Tilt Matching
item.description: arXiv:2604.18739v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion large language models (dLLMs) are a promising alternative to autoregressive generation. While reinforcement learning (RL) methods have recently been adapted to dLLM fine-tuning, their objectives typically depend on sequence-level marg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, arxiv, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Coefficients: Forecast-Necessity Testing for Interpretable Causal Discovery in Nonlinear Time-Series Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.18751v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Nonlinear machine-learning models are increasingly used to discover causal relationships in time-series data, yet the interpretation of their outputs remains poorly understood. In particular, causal scores produced by regularized neural autoregressive...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, paper, vector, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Understanding the Robustness of Sparse Autoencoders
item.description: arXiv:2604.18756v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) remain vulnerable to optimization-based jailbreak attacks that exploit internal gradient structure. While Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) are widely used for interpretability, their robustness implications remain underexplored....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, augmented, study, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Probing for Reading Times
item.description: arXiv:2604.18712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Probing has shown that language model representations encode rich linguistic information, but it remains unclear whether they also capture cognitive signals about human processing. In this work, we probe language model representations for human readin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond One Output: Visualizing and Comparing Distributions of Language Model Generations
item.description: arXiv:2604.18724v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Users typically interact with and evaluate language models via single outputs, but each output is just one sample from a broad distribution of possible completions. This interaction hides distributional structure such as modes, uncommon edge cases, an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, research, prompt, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Quantum inspired qubit qutrit neural networks for real time financial forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2604.18838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This research investigates the performance and efficacy of machine learning models in stock prediction, comparing Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), Quantum Qubit-based Neural Networks (QQBNs), and Quantum Qutrit-based Neural Networks (QQTNs). By outl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Cost of Relaxation: Evaluating the Error in Convex Neural Network Verification
item.description: arXiv:2604.18728v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many neural network (NN) verification systems represent the network's input-output relation as a constraint program. Sound and complete, representations involve integer constraints, for simulating the activations. Recent works convexly relax the integ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
